task_completion.description: Align the 8 sequences using the MUSCLE algorithm
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing the Multiple Sequence Alignment window with the MUSCLE dialog open, or the resulting aligned sequences displayed on screen.

task_completion.description: Export the alignment to cytc_alignment.sto in Stockholm format
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export dialog showing the Stockholm format selected and the file path ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytc_alignment.sto, or a file manager/terminal showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Identify the conserved heme-binding motif using the pattern C..CH
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE 'Find Pattern' or search dialog showing the regular expression 'C..CH' being applied to the sequences, with matches found.
UGENE annotations table or sequence view showing the matched regions highlighted and explicitly labeled with the name 'heme_binding_site'.

task_completion.description: Export the annotations to cytc_heme_motifs.gff in GFF format
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export annotations dialog showing GFF format selected and the file path ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytc_heme_motifs.gff, or visual confirmation of the file's creation.

task_completion.description: Generate a summary report including sequence names, motif presence, start coordinates, and a conclusion
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the contents of ~/UGENE_Data/results/heme_motif_report.txt, which must include a list of sequence names, their motif start coordinates, and a brief text conclusion on conservation.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE graphical interface to perform the alignment and motif search
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with UGENE menus, dialogs, and sequence viewers rather than just running terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent extracted real coordinates from the UGENE results rather than hallucinating them
integrity.visual_evidence: The coordinates typed into the text report match the ones visible in the UGENE annotation/search results window.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create dummy output files via terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The .sto and .gff files are generated via UGENE's export dialogs, not by using 'touch' or 'echo' in the terminal to create empty or fake files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FASTA file contains 8 real UniProt Cytochrome c sequences (e.g., P99999, P67881). All 8 sequences contain exactly the universally conserved heme-binding motif 'C..CH'. The expected output files are 'cytc_alignment.sto', 'cytc_heme_motifs.gff', and 'heme_motif_report.txt' located in '~/UGENE_Data/results/'.
